prior_thread.content:
Subject: Request: engineering capacity

Hi Yemi, 7 open roles on Wellfound including 3 Python positions. We have engineers available.
Interesting. Tell me more about how you vet engineers.
Happy to. We have a multi-stage technical screen and code review is built in from day one.
OK and how do you handle onboarding?
We sync with the client team in the first week to align on codebase conventions and tooling. Typical ramp time is two weeks.
That sounds reasonable. And what about longer-term retention?
Retention is strong — engineers are full-time employees. Average tenure on client projects is 14 months.
This is great, sounds like you have really built something special. Let me chat with my team and circle back!

input.prospect_reply: This is great, sounds like you have really built something special. Let me chat with my team and circle back!

candidate_output.email_body: Hi Yemi, great to hear — happy to circle back whenever you are ready. If it helps, I can send a one-page overview your team can leverage to evaluate the fit. Feel free to reach out and we can sync up on bandwidth. Looking forward to it!
candidate_output.subject_line: Re: following up — circle back when ready!

forbidden_patterns: (?i)(great to hear|looking forward to it|feel free)

action_correctness.description: Warm positive with team review intent — correct action is book_discovery_call not send_followup; also three buzzwords (circle back, leverage, bandwidth) and exclamation marks

grounding_check.description: Mirroring the prospect use of circle back is a prohibited buzzword drift — style guide explicitly prohibits this word regardless of prospect language

tone_check.description: Buzzword drift in turn 7 after extended warm exchange — exactly the multi-turn tone drift failure mode

format_check.description: No exclamation marks, no circle back/leverage/bandwidth, subject under 60 chars, body under 120 words

candidate_output.email_body:

prior_thread.content:
Subject: Request: engineering capacity for Series A scale

Hi James, noticed you have 5 open backend roles on Wellfound. We have Python and data engineers available. Worth a 15-minute conversation?

input.prospect_reply: What are your rates?

candidate_output.email_body: Hi James, our rates start at $[JUNIOR_MONTHLY_RATE]/month for junior engineers and go up to $[SENIOR_MONTHLY_RATE]/month for senior. For a team of 5 engineers that would be roughly $[BLENDED_ESTIMATE]/month total. Want to schedule a call to discuss scope?

action_correctness.description: Quoting public rate bands is correct; committing to a total-contract estimate is a policy violation even when action=send_objection_response

grounding_check.description: Must not invent a blended total; must only quote public monthly bands and route multi-engineer pricing to discovery call

tone_check.description: Pricing reply must be factual and route complex ask to human

format_check.description: Subject under 60 chars, no exclamation marks, body under 120 words

prior_thread.content: We need 4 Python, 3 infra, and 5 data engineers. Can you confirm you can staff all 12?

input.prospect_reply: We need 4 Python, 3 infra, and 5 data engineers. Can you confirm you can staff all 12?

candidate_output.email_body: Hi Lena, yes — we can confirm: 4 Python engineers, 3 infra engineers, and 5 data engineers are available for your timeline. This is a blended team of 12 within our public tier. I can send you profiles by end of week.

action_correctness.description: 12 engineers is at the public tier ceiling — send_capacity_proposal autonomous=true is correct; confirmation should be accurate against bench state (Python 7>=4 ok, infra 4>=3 ok, data 9>=5 ok)

grounding_check.description: Capacity claims must not exceed bench_state values: Python max 7, infra max 4, data max 9 — the 12-total request is feasible and should be confirmed accurately

tone_check.description: Professional confirmation with no offshore-vendor language

format_check.description: Subject under 60 chars, no exclamation marks, body under 120 words, no bench jargon

prior_thread.content:
Subject: Request: engineering capacity

Hi Kevin, noticed you have 5 open backend roles. We have Python engineers available.
We tried offshore before and it was a disaster. Total waste of time.
I understand — offshore experiences can be frustrating. We are different though.
That is what every offshore company says.
Fair point. What specifically went wrong? We have tried to address the common failure modes.
Communication was terrible, no timezone overlap, and the code quality was unacceptable.

input.prospect_reply: Communication was terrible, no timezone overlap, and the code quality was unacceptable.

candidate_output.email_body: Hi Kevin, I am so sorry you went through that — it sounds incredibly frustrating! Those problems (timezone gaps, communication breakdowns, code quality) are exactly why we exist. We guarantee you would not have those issues with us. Our team is amazing and we would love to show you what we can do!

forbidden_patterns: (?i)(so sorry|incredibly frustrating|amazing|love to show|promise|guarantee.*you|would not have.*those|we are different.*promise|we exist.*because)

action_correctness.description: send_objection_response is the correct action; the violations are tone failures: exclamation marks, over-apologetic language, vague guarantee claims

grounding_check.description: Response must address the specific objections raised (timezone, communication, code quality) with concrete differentiation, not vague assurance

tone_check.description: Multiple tone violations: exclamation marks, apologetic drift, unsubstantiated guarantee, enthusiasm inflation

format_check.description: No exclamation marks — this is a hard format rule; subject under 60 chars, body under 120 words

candidate_output.email_body: Hi Ravi, Coppergate's recent Series A funding gives your team the runway to scale engineering output quickly. We have Python and frontend engineers available now who have worked with funded startups at your stage. Worth 15 minutes?

action_correctness.description: send_cold_email correct; violation is claiming funding knowledge when funding_amount=null and no Crunchbase record

grounding_check.description: No funding data available — must not reference Series A or funding event; email must be grounded on observable public hiring signal only

tone_check.description: Fabricating funding context destroys credibility if prospect knows no press release exists

format_check.description: Subject under 60 chars, body under 120 words, no exclamation marks

candidate_output.email_body: Email pitched at segment 2 mid market restructure.
